preamble: The transformation of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into the mid front vowel [e] is typically an indirect phonetic shift mediated by rhotacism, where the fricative first shifts to a rhotic and subsequently vocalizes or influences adjacent vowel quality.

examples.note: Historical rhotacism where voiced sibilants became rhotics, which in specific environments influenced the height and frontness of surrounding vowels.
Development of voiced fricatives into rhotics followed by vocalization or influence on vowel quality in unstressed syllables.

sources: Phonological research on the perceptual link between [z] and [r].
